frameworks.name: Bayesian sensorimotor integration
frameworks.one_line: Combines prior with sensory feedback to estimate motor variables.

frameworks.name: naive single-prior Bayesian observer
frameworks.one_line: Predicts one veridical prior shared across effectors.

core_question: Does the sensorimotor prior depend on which effector indicates the estimate?
key_assumptions: Prior and likelihood are combined according to Bayes rule
Prior and feedback uncertainty quantitatively determine reliance weights
Effector identity could in principle leave the prior invariant
manipulations: Effector used for the estimate (arm reaching vs wrist rotation)
key_findings: Prior and feedback uncertainty modulate reliance as Bayes predicts in both effectors
Reliance on the prior is larger for reaching than for wrist rotation
Single-prior Bayesian account is violated across effectors
interpretations: Sensorimotor priors are constructed in an effector-dependent way
The brain is not naively Bayesian with one veridical prior
